Article.ArticleTitle: Equal-power Ho:YAG laser energy-frequency modulation during RIRS for renal pelvis stones: 0.3 J × 50 Hz vs. 1.5 J × 10 Hz.

AffiliationInfo.Affiliation: Department of Urology, Van Training and Research Hospital, Van, Turkey. dokterms@gmail.com.
Department of Urology, Van Training and Research Hospital, Van, Turkey.
Department of Urology, Van Training and Research Hospital, Van, Turkey.
Department of Urology, Van Training and Research Hospital, Van, Turkey.

MedlineCitation.CoiStatement: Declarations. Competing interests: The authors declare no competing interests. Ethics approval: The study was approved by the local ethics committee (Approval No. 2025-09-27) and conducted in accordance with the Declaration of Helsinki. Consent to participate: Due to the retrospective design and the use of anonymized data obtained during routine clinical care, informed consent was waived in accordance with institutional policy.

Reference.Citation: Ramello A, Vitale C, Marangella M (2001) Epidemiology of nephrolithiasis. J Nephrol 13(Suppl):S45–S50
Sorokin I, Mamoulakis C, Miyazawa K et al (2017) Epidemiology of stone disease across the world. World J Urol 35(9):1301–1320. https://doi.org/10.1007/s00345-017-2008-6

Erhard MJ, Bagley DH (1995) Urologic applications of the holmium laser: preliminary experience. J Endourol 9(5):383–386. https://doi.org/10.1089/end.1995.9.383
Coptcoat M, Webb DR, Kellet MJ et al (1988) Lasertripsy for ureteric stones in 120 cases: lessons learned. Br J Urol 61(6):487–489. https://doi.org/10.1111/j.1464-410X.1988.tb05085.x
Knudsen BE (2019) Laser fibers for holmium:YAG lithotripsy: what is important and what is new. Urol Clin North Am 46(2):193–203. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.ucl.2018.12.004

Kam J, Yeo D, Lee J et al (2020) Long pulse-width setting improves holmium:YAG laser lithotripsy-enhanced fragmentation and retropulsion characteristics of renal calculi in a novel in vivo setting. Surg Technol Int 37:168–170

Elshazly M, El-Hefnawy AS, Elsobky E et al (2024) Comparison of high and low pulse energy dusting protocols using holmium:YAG laser in flexible ureteroscopy for treatment of renal stones. Arab J Urol 22(3):186–190. https://doi.org/10.1080/20905998.2024.2343181

Pareek G, Hedican SP, Lee FT Jr et al (2005) Extracorporeal shock wave lithotripsy success based on body mass index and Hounsfield units. Urology 65(1):33–36. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.urology.2004.08.004
